info.name: Qualiphy API
info.description: Qualiphy telemedicine API. Authentication: every request body MUST include an `api_key` field (no headers, no query params). All endpoints are POST with `Content-Type: application/json`. Base URL defaults to https://api.qualiphy.me. Set `api_key` in the collection or environment variables before running.

item.name: Exam
item.description: Patient exam invites and lifecycle endpoints.

request.description: Returns every exam configured on your clinic. Use this to discover the `id` values you pass to `/exam_invite`. Each exam record includes `rx_type` (1=GFE, 2=Rx), `attachments_required`, `attachments`, and a preset `price` if configured.

Core endpoint. Creates a patient exam invite, returns a meeting URL, and (by default) emails and texts the patient. This example uses test-mode routing (`state` and `tele_state` set to TE) so it is safe to fire as-is. For Rx flows, add `pharmacy_id` + `order_set_id` (partner pharmacy) or `custom_pharmacy: 1` + `ncpdpid` + custom-pharmacy address fields. Watch the `custom_pharmacy` mapping — 1=Custom, 2=Partner.

Re-sends the invite email and SMS to the patient. Use when a patient says they did not receive their original invite. `patient_exam_id` comes from the `patient_exams[]` array of the original `/exam_invite` response.

Cancel a previously created patient exam invite. Valid only before the provider has acted. Pass `patient_exam_id` (not `exam_id`). Returns 400 with status detail if the exam is already approved, rejected, or deferred — treat duplicate cancellations as success in retry logic.
Returns the screening question schema for the given exam. Use to build pre-fill UI on your own intake form, or to map your existing intake fields onto the Qualiphy question set.

Submits answers on behalf of the patient. Pair with `/exam_questions` to pre-fill the screening using your own intake data. If you submit answers for every question, the questionnaire is skipped entirely and the patient goes straight to the provider call.

Attach a document to a patient exam (ID photos, lab results, prior records). The provider sees attachments during the screening. Note: the public docs section for this endpoint does not enumerate parameters; the fields here are a best-effort reconstruction. Confirm exact field names with Qualiphy before going live.

item.name: Pharmacy
item.description: Partner pharmacy and custom pharmacy discovery endpoints.

request.description: Lists Qualiphy partner compounding pharmacies available for your account. Use the returned `pharmacy_id` with `/exam_invite` and `/partner_pharmacy_treatment_packages`.

Lists treatment packages (drug + dosage + pricing) for a specific partner pharmacy and Rx exam. Returns `order_set_id` for use with `/exam_invite`. Legacy `exam_pos_id` is still returned for older integrations.

Look up any U.S. pharmacy by name, ZIP, or NCPDPID. Returns the full address and identifier fields needed for `/exam_invite` custom-pharmacy routing. Pass `ncpdpid` alone to fetch a known pharmacy by ID.

item.name: Webhook (reference)
item.description: Reference-only documentation of the inbound webhook Qualiphy POSTs to your `webhook_url`. These are NOT requests you fire — they document what your handler should expect. Dispatch on the `event` field: 1=Consultation Complete, 2=Prescription Confirmed (Rx only), 3=Prescription Tracking (partner pharmacy shipping).

body.raw:
{
  "event": 1,
  "reason": "Initial consultation complete",
  "exam_id": "exam-001",
  "exam_url": "https://d29tu1ox8kfrdk.cloudfront.net/pdfs/...",
  "exam_name": "General Health Check",
  "exam_status": "Approved",
  "rx_status": "Pharmacy Confirmed",
  "clinic_name": "Downtown Medical Center",
  "patient_email": "patient@example.com",
  "patient_phone_number": "5551234567",
  "patient_exam_id": "patient-exam-789",
  "provider_name": "NP Liza",
  "additional_data": "{\"order_id\":\"WC-12847\"}",
  "questions_answers": [
    { "no": 1, "question": "How are you feeling?", "answer": "Good." }
  ]
}

request.description:
REFERENCE ONLY — DO NOT SEND.

Qualiphy POSTs lifecycle events to your `webhook_url`. Your handler should respond 200 immediately and process asynchronously.

Dispatch on `event`:

- event=1 Consultation Complete. Fires for every exam (GFE, Rx, Urgent Care). `exam_status` is one of: Approved, Rejected, Deferred to Medical Director, NA, Missed. `exam_url` is a CloudFront-hosted signed PDF — mirror to your own storage if you need long-term access.

- event=2 Prescription Confirmed. Fires only for Rx exams after event 1. Carries `prescription[]` (each item: drug_name, dosage_form_type, strength, size, quantity, directions, duration_days, refill_amount, ndc_number, schedule_code, pharmacy_name, pharmacy_contacts[], prescription_tracking[], created_at), `prescribed_os_id`, and the full consultation envelope.

- event=3 Prescription Tracking. Fires only when a partner pharmacy ships. Carries `patient_exam_id`, `prescription_id`, `tracking_number`, `delivery_service`, `pharmacy_location`, `order_id`. Custom-pharmacy flows never get event 3.

This example shows an event=1 Approved payload. Switch the body to event 2 or 3 to model the other shapes.
